        have h₆ : ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
          -- Use the fact that the norm of the integral is bounded by ‖x‖ * ‖y‖
          calc
            ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral‖ = ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y)‖ := by
              simp [ComplexMeasure.norm_def]
            _ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
              -- Use the fact that the norm of the integral is bounded by ‖x‖ * ‖y‖
              have h₇ : ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y)‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
                -- Use the given bound on the norm of the integral
                have h₈ : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound x y
                have h₉ : ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y)‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
                  -- Use the given bound on the norm of the integral
                  simpa [ComplexMeasure.norm_def] using h₈
                exact h₉
              simpa using h₇
        exact h₆
      exact h₄
    -- Combine the inequalities to get the final result
    calc
      ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral‖ * C := h₂
      _ ≤ (‖x‖ * ‖y‖) * C := by
        -- Use the fact that the norm of the integral is bounded by ‖x‖ * ‖y‖
        gcongr
        <;> simp_all [Complex.abs_def, Real.norm_eq_abs]
        <;> linarith
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * C := by ring
  exact h₁

have h₁ : ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * C := by
    have h₂ : ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * C := by
      have h₃ : ∀ (x : ↑(spectrum ℂ a)), ‖f x‖ ≤ C := hf_bounded
      have h₄ : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f := hX
      have h₅ : ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * C := by
        -- Use the properties of the complex measure and the boundedness of f to bound the integral
        have h₆ : ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * C := by
          -- Apply the boundedness condition to the integral
          calc
            ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
              -- Use the fact that the integral of f against the measure is bounded by the norm of f
              have h₇ : ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
                -- Use the fact that the integral of f against the measure is bounded by the norm of f
                sorry
              exact h₇
            _ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * C := by
              -- Use the fact that the norm of f is bounded by C
              have h₉ : ‖f‖ ≤ C := by
                -- Use the fact that the norm of f is bounded by C
                sorry
              have h₁₀ : (‖f‖ : ℝ) ≤ C := by exact_mod_cast h₉
              have h₁₁ : (‖x‖ * ‖y‖ : ℝ) ≥ 0 := by positivity
              have h₁₂ : (‖x‖ * ‖y‖ : ℝ) * ‖f‖ ≤ (‖x‖ * ‖y‖ : ℝ) * C := by
                -- Multiply both sides by the non-negative quantity ‖x‖ * ‖y‖
                nlinarith
              -- Cast the result back to the appropriate type
              exact_mod_cast h₁₂
        exact h₆
      exact h₅
    exact h₂
  exact h₁

have h1 : ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * C := by
    have h2 : ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * C := by
      have h3 : ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * C := by
        have h4 : ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * C := by
          -- Use the fact that the norm of the integral is bounded by the product of the norms of x and y and the bound C
          have h5 : ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * C := by
            calc
              ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ = ‖(compSL (C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ))
                  C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)
                  (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE
                    (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap).integral f‖ := rfl
              _ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * C := by
                -- Use the given bounds and properties of the integral to establish the inequality
                have h6 : ‖(compSL (C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ))
                    C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)
                    (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE
                      (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap)‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
                  -- Use the bound on the linear functional
                  have h7 : ‖(compSL (C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ))
                      C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)
                      (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE
                        (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap)‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
                    calc
                      ‖(compSL (C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ))
                          C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)
                          (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE
                            (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap)‖
                      = ‖(compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)
                          (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE
                            (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap)‖ := by rfl
                      _ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
                        -- Use the bound on the linear functional and the fact that cfcCLM is an isometry
                        calc
                          ‖(compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)
                              (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE
                                (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap)‖
                          = ‖(toLinearFunctional x y) ∘ₗ[ℂ] (cfcCLM ha) ∘ₗ[ℂ] (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE
                              (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap‖ := by
                              rfl
                          _ ≤ ‖(toLinearFunctional x y) ∘ₗ[ℂ] (cfcCLM ha)‖ := by
                              apply ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_comp_le_of_norm_lt_one_right
                              <;>
                              (try norm_num) <;>
                              (try
                                {
                                  refine' le_of_eq _
                                  <;> simp [ContinuousLinearMap.norm_def]
                                  <;>
                                  (try norm_num)
                                  <;>
                                  (try
                                    {
                                      simp_all [ContinuousLinearMap.norm_def, ContinuousLinearMap.le_opNorm]

                                      norm_num
                                      <;>
                                      linarith
                                    })
                                }) <;>
                              (try
                                {
                                  simp_all [ContinuousLinearMap.norm_def, ContinuousLinearMap.le_opNorm]
                                  <;>
                                  norm_num
                                  <;>
                                  linarith
                                })
                          _ = ‖(toLinearFunctional x y) ∘ₗ[ℂ] (cfcCLM ha)‖ := by rfl
                          _ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
                            -- Use the bound on the linear functional and the fact that cfcCLM is an isometry
                            calc
                              ‖(toLinearFunctional x y) ∘ₗ[ℂ] (cfcCLM ha)‖
                              = ‖toLinearFunctional x y‖ * ‖cfcCLM ha‖ := by

                                (try simp_all [ContinuousLinearMap.norm_def, ContinuousLinearMap.le_opNorm]) <;>

                                -- The norm of cfcCLM is 1
                                have h8 : ‖(cfcCLM ha : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H)‖ = 1 := by

                                rw [h8]

                              _ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
                                -- The norm of toLinearFunctional is bounded by ‖x‖ * ‖y‖
                                have h9 : ‖(toLinearFunctional x y : (H →L[ℂ] H) →L[ℂ] ℂ)‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by

                                exact h9
                  exact h7
                -- Use the bound on the linear functional to bound the integral
                have h10 : ‖(compSL (C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ))
                    C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)
                    (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE

                -- Use the fact that the norm of the integral is bounded by the norm of the linear functional

                        (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap)‖ * C := by

                    gcongr
